interviewStages: Behavioral: ownership, on-call discipline, regulated-industry care
codingTopics: OO design: model a business domain such as workflows with triggers and actions
Multi-tenant data structures: per-tenant rate limiting, isolation
Streaming: rolling aggregates over tenant events
Graph and DAG: workflow evaluation, dependency resolution
Search and filters: AND/OR queries over records
systemDesignTopics: Design a global SIP/WebRTC platform with five-nines uptime
Design call routing across regional PoPs
Design a real-time analytics pipeline for call quality

coding: At RingCentral, model a workflow engine with triggers, conditions, and actions. Evaluate a workflow DAG and detect cycles.
Implement a per-tenant rate limiter so one customer cannot exhaust shared capacity. Discuss the token-bucket design.
Given a stream of tenant events, maintain rolling per-entity scores with bounded memory.
Implement a filter query over records supporting AND/OR on custom fields. Discuss indexing for a multi-tenant store.
Deduplicate records via fuzzy clustering on name and email. Discuss the matching and complexity.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at RingCentral scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows ownership, on-call discipline, regulated-industry care.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a global SIP/WebRTC platform with five-nines uptime.
Design call routing across regional PoPs.
Design a real-time analytics pipeline for call quality.
values: RingCentral succeeds when its customers do. Describe a time you solved for the customer even at a cost to the team.
Tell me about a time you handled a tricky multi-tenant isolation or permissions problem.
How do you balance enterprise customization against product simplicity?
other: How does RingCentral compare to Zoom or 8×8?

rawSummary:
RingCentral Interview Guide (2026): Cloud Communications Engineering
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 6, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 6, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

RingCentral
ringcentral.com ↗

RingCentral is the largest pure-play UCaaS provider — voice, video, messaging, contact center. Public since 2013. The interview emphasizes real-time media (SIP, WebRTC, codecs), high-availability voice infrastructure, and the unique challenges of regulated voice traffic at carrier scale.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–5 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a global SIP/WebRTC platform with five-nines uptime

- Design call routing across regional PoPs

- Design a real-time analytics pipeline for call quality

- Coding: medium DSA, often with networking or concurrency framing

- Behavioral: ownership, on-call discipline, regulated-industry care

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $130K–$165K total

- Senior SE: $190K–$250K

- Staff: $275K–$370K

- Principal: $390K–$510K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Java, Go, or C++ (split across product lines)

- Understand SIP/RTP, WebRTC, and audio codecs

- Brush up on telecom regulation (E911, STIR/SHAKEN)

Frequently Asked Questions

Is RingCentral remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Belmont CA (HQ), Charlotte, Denver, plus international. Many engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does RingCentral compare to Zoom or 8×8?

Zoom is video-first and now expanding to voice. 8×8 is the smaller competitor. RingCentral is the voice-first incumbent. Comp is mid-tier; below cloud leaders.

What is the engineering culture?

Mature, telecom-grade, calmer pace. Strong work-life balance.
